𝗪𝗘𝗖𝗟𝗢𝗡𝗘 | To create a personalized AI digital avatar that mimics a user's communication style by fine-tuning an LLM on their chat history.  🔥 Super Hot!

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To create a personalized AI digital avatar that mimics a user's communication style by fine-tuning an LLM on their chat history.
📅 Created: Jan 2024 • Updated: Apr 2026

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ End-to-end solution for creating digital avatars
✅ Chat data export and preprocessing
✅ LLM fine-tuning with chat history (including image data)
✅ Integration with Telegram and other platforms (WhatsApp, Discord, Slack, WeChat)
✅ Privacy information filtering
✅ Localized fine-tuning and deployment


▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 17.2K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 1.4K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 35 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

WeClone is a Python-based solution for creating a personalized AI digital avatar from chat history. It allows users to fine-tune Large Language Models (LLMs) with their chat logs, capturing their unique communication style, and then deploy this model as a chatbot, effectively bringing their digital self to life.

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/xming521/WeClone

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📅 Created: Sep 2025 • Updated: Apr 2026

✅ Lua API for configuration

✅ LSP-powered autocomplete
✅ Sane defaults
✅ NixOS integration
✅ AUR package for Arch Linux

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

OXWM is a dynamic window manager written in Zig, offering an enhanced alternative to dwm. It provides a flexible Lua API for configuration with hot-reloading, aiming to reduce user friction through sensible defaults, LSP integration, and instant configuration updates without restarting the X session.

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/tonybanters/oxwm

𝗙𝗨𝗡-𝗔𝗦𝗥 | Accurate and efficient speech-to-text transcription, especially in specialized domains and multilingual environments.  🔥 Super Hot!

✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Accurate and efficient speech-to-text transcription, especially in specialized domains and multilingual environments.
📅 Created: Dec 2025 • Updated: Apr 2026

✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ End-to-end speech recognition large model
✅ Trained on tens of millions of hours of real speech data
✅ Powerful contextual understanding capabilities
✅ Industry adaptability (education, finance)
✅ Low-latency real-time transcription
✅ Supports 31 languages
✅ Accurate recognition of professional terminology and industry expressions
✅ Addresses 'hallucination' generation and language confusion
✅ Supports 7 Chinese dialects and 26 regional accents
✅ Covers multiple regional accents for English and Japanese
✅ Lyric recognition and rap speech recognition


✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿

🍴 95 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 59 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Fun-ASR is an advanced, end-to-end speech recognition large model developed by Tongyi Lab. It is trained on massive datasets, offering robust contextual understanding and industry-specific adaptability. The model supports low-latency real-time transcription across 31 languages and excels in specialized domains by accurately handling jargon and minimizing errors like hallucinations.

✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿

𝗡𝗔𝗡𝗢-𝗣𝗗𝗙 | Editing PDF documents, particularly presentations or reports, by describing desired changes in natural language, making PDF modification more accessible and efficient.  🔥 Super Hot!

✅ Add New Slides
✅ Non-Destructive Editing (OCR Re-hydration)

🍴 82 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Nano-PDF is a Python-based command-line tool that allows users to edit PDF documents using natural language prompts. It leverages Google's Gemini 3 Pro Image model to understand and execute edits, such as updating text or generating new content, while preserving the original document's structure and text layer through OCR.

𝗣𝗚𝗦𝗖𝗛𝗘𝗠𝗔 | Automating and simplifying PostgreSQL schema migrations using a declarative, Terraform-like approach, especially for complex schemas with advanced PostgreSQL features.  🔥 Super Hot!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐹 Go 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Automating and simplifying PostgreSQL schema migrations using a declarative, Terraform-like approach, especially for complex schemas with advanced PostgreSQL features.
📅 Created: Jun 2025 • Updated: Apr 2026

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Terraform-style declarative schema migration
✅ Automatic DDL generation from desired state
✅ Support for advanced PostgreSQL-specific objects (RLS, partitioning, triggers, etc.)
✅ No migration history table required
✅ No shadow database required
✅ Plan/preview step before applying changes
✅ Concurrent change detection
✅ Transaction-adaptive execution
✅ Lock timeout control


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 886 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 40 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 7 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

pgschema is a Go-based CLI tool that enables Terraform-style declarative schema migrations for PostgreSQL. It allows users to define their desired database schema state and automatically generates the necessary SQL migration DDL, simplifying the migration process and supporting advanced PostgreSQL features.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/pgplex/pgschema
